response:
I'll perform cell type deconvolution on slice 0 using RCTD with your reference dataset. This will decompose each spatial spot into proportions of different cell types from the single-cell reference.

```python
import scanpy as sc
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Load spatial data (slice 0)
slice = session.get_slice(0)
ad_spatial = slice.adata.copy()

# Verify it's spot-level data
if not slice.is_spot_level:
    raise ValueError("Deconvolution requires spot-level data (Visium)")

print(f"Spatial data: {ad_spatial.n_obs} spots × {ad_spatial.n_vars} genes")

# Load reference single-cell data
ad_sc = sc.read_h5ad('/Users/vvv/Code/Claude-project/STAT-agent/paper/benchmarking/comparison/results/D_stat/Q18/run_0/dlpfc_reference.h5ad')

print(f"Reference data: {ad_sc.n_obs} cells × {ad_sc.n_vars} genes")
print(f"Cell types in reference: {ad_sc.obs['celltype'].value_counts()}")

# Make sure gene/cell names are unique
ad_spatial.var_names_make_unique()
ad_sc.var_names_make_unique()
ad_spatial.obs_names_make_unique()
ad_sc.obs_names_make_unique()

# Check gene overlap
common_genes = set(ad_spatial.var_names) & set(ad_sc.var_names)
print(f"Common genes between spatial and reference: {len(common_genes)}")
```

```python
# Step 2: Select markers in the reference
ad_sc.raw = ad_sc.copy()
sc.pp.normalize_total(ad_sc, target_sum=2000)

sc.pp.highly_variable_genes(ad_sc, flavor='seurat_v3', n_top_genes=1000)
sc.tl.rank_genes_groups(ad_sc, groupby="celltype", method='wilcoxon')
markers_df = pd.DataFrame(ad_sc.uns["rank_genes_groups"]["names"]).iloc[0:100, :]
markers = list(np.unique(markers_df.melt().value.values))
markers = list(set(ad_sc.var.loc[ad_sc.var['highly_variable']==1].index)|set(markers))
ad_sc.var.loc[ad_sc.var.index.isin(markers),'Marker'] = True
ad_sc.var['Marker'] = ad_sc.var['Marker'].fillna(False)

ad_sc.var['highly_variable'] = ad_sc.var['Marker']
sel_genes = ad_sc.var.index[ad_sc.var['Marker']]

print(f"Selected {len(sel_genes)} marker genes for deconvolution")

# Only preserve these markers in spatial data
ad_spatial = ad_spatial[:,ad_spatial.var.index.isin(sel_genes)]
print(f"Filtered spatial data: {ad_spatial.n_obs} spots × {ad_spatial.n_vars} genes")
```

```
Selected 2386 marker genes for deconvolution
Filtered spatial data: 3611 spots × 1765 genes

```
```python
# Step 3: Run RCTD deconvolution
from deconv_rctd import SpatialRNA, Reference, create_RCTD, run_RCTD

print("Preparing data for RCTD...")

# Prepare spatial data for RCTD
counts_spatial = ad_spatial.to_df().T
coords = ad_spatial.obs[['x', 'y']]
nUMI_spatial = pd.DataFrame(np.array(ad_spatial.X.sum(-1)),
                            index=ad_spatial.obs.index)
puck = SpatialRNA(coords, counts_spatial, nUMI_spatial)

# Prepare reference data for RCTD
counts_ref = ad_sc.to_df().T
cell_types_ref = pd.DataFrame(ad_sc.obs['celltype'])
nUMI_ref = pd.DataFrame(ad_sc.to_df().T.sum(0))
reference = Reference(counts_ref, cell_types_ref, nUMI_ref)

print("Running RCTD deconvolution...")
# Run RCTD
myRCTD = create_RCTD(puck, reference, max_cores=20)
myRCTD = run_RCTD(myRCTD)

print("RCTD deconvolution completed!")

# IMPORTANT: Shutdown Ray to free resources
import ray
if ray.is_initialized():
    ray.shutdown()

# IMPORTANT: Reset numba JIT state after Ray shutdown
try:
    import numba.core.registry
    numba.core.registry.cpu_target.typing_context.refresh()
    numba.core.registry.cpu_target.target_context.refresh()
except Exception:
    pass
```

```python
# Step 4: Update session data with deconvolution results
print("Updating session data with deconvolution results...")

# Get filtered spots from RCTD results
filtered_spots = myRCTD["results"].index
print(f"RCTD kept {len(filtered_spots)} out of {slice.adata.n_obs} original spots")

# Update session adata to only include filtered spots
slice.adata = slice.adata[filtered_spots, :].copy()

# Add deconvolution weights to obsm
slice.adata.obsm["deconv_weights"] = myRCTD["results"]
slice.adata.uns['has_deconv_weights'] = True

# Update the dominant celltype
dominant_celltypes = slice.adata.obsm["deconv_weights"].idxmax(axis=1)
slice.adata.obs['celltype'] = dominant_celltypes

print("Deconvolution results summary:")
print(f"Final data: {slice.adata.n_obs} spots × {slice.adata.n_vars} genes")
print(f"Cell types found: {slice.adata.obsm['deconv_weights'].columns.tolist()}")
print(f"\nDominant cell type distribution:")
print(slice.adata.obs['celltype'].value_counts())
print(f"\nMean proportions across all spots:")
print(slice.adata.obsm['deconv_weights'].mean().round(3))
```

```python
# Set matplotlib backend for thread safety
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Get slice 0 data
slice = session.get_slice(0)

# Plot dominant cell types spatially
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 8))
celltype_colors = plt.cm.Set3(np.linspace(0, 1, len(slice.adata.obs['celltype'].unique())))
celltype_map = dict(zip(slice.adata.obs['celltype'].unique(), celltype_colors))

for celltype in slice.adata.obs['celltype'].unique():
    mask = slice.adata.obs['celltype'] == celltype
    plt.scatter(slice.adata.obs.loc[mask, 'x'], 
                slice.adata.obs.loc[mask, 'y'],
                c=[celltype_map[celltype]], 
                label=celltype, s=20, alpha=0.8)

plt.legend(bbox_to_anchor=(1.05, 1), loc='upper left')
plt.title('Spatial Distribution of Dominant Cell Types')
plt.xlabel('X coordinate')
plt.ylabel('Y coordinate')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

*(1 plot(s) generated)*
```python
# Plot proportions for top cell types
top_celltypes = slice.adata.obsm['deconv_weights'].mean().nlargest(4).index

fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 2, figsize=(15, 12))
axes = axes.flatten()

for i, celltype in enumerate(top_celltypes):
    proportions = slice.adata.obsm['deconv_weights'][celltype]
    
    im = axes[i].scatter(slice.adata.obs['x'], slice.adata.obs['y'],
                        c=proportions, s=20, cmap='viridis', alpha=0.8)
    axes[i].set_title(f'{celltype} Proportions')
    axes[i].set_xlabel('X coordinate')
    axes[i].set_ylabel('Y coordinate')
    plt.colorbar(im, ax=axes[i], label='Proportion')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

*(1 plot(s) generated)*

**Analysis:**
The deconvolution successfully identified cell type proportions in each spatial spot. Focus on: (1) how many spots were retained after quality filtering, (2) the diversity of cell types detected, (3) spatial patterns in the dominant cell type map, and (4) whether certain cell types show spatial clustering or gradients in their proportions.
